verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: bwrap wrapper builds via flake; No host installs required; Pinned inputs; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red against the pre-delivery surface (engine absent: 7/7 files failing) at lifeos red commit, green at HEAD (2/2)
gate_result.build_evidence: nix build .#yzx-envelope on the yazelix flake (branch archbp-065-071-isolation-envelope, PR FlexNetOS/yazelix#103) produces /nix/store/dwvgys4ykl1sap674fpnjs5zkspq206g-yzx-envelope; the built binary runs and emits its executor JSON.
gate_result.hermetic_evidence: writeShellApplication with runtimeInputs = [bubblewrap coreutils gnugrep] — the build needs nothing from the host; shellcheck passes at build time.
gate_result.pinning_evidence: bubblewrap comes from the flake's pinned nixpkgs input (pkgs.bubblewrap), never from the host; the flake app apps.<system>.yzx-envelope exposes the engine.
gate_result.executor_evidence: On this host the store bwrap is denied userns by kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=1; the engine records the downgrade to the AppArmor-profiled /usr/bin/bwrap with root cause and the owner-gated permanent fix (an AppArmor profile for the store bwrap) — never silent.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The spec performs the real nix build and executes the built store binary.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-065 is complete: the envelope is nix-declared — flake package + app yzx-envelope (writeShellApplication, pinned bubblewrap input) builds hermetically with no host installs and the built store binary runs; executor selection honestly records the AppArmor-profiled host-bwrap fallback on restricted hosts instead of silently downgrading.
